constraints: avoid running and jumping for a full year
decisions: prioritize steady-state, low-impact 27-minute sessions for beginners
introduction of one low-impact interval session per week after surgeon clearance
start with a small core set of 4–8 exercises and run consistently for several weeks before adding variety; swap one exercise at a time when introducing new moves
entities: equipment: two 4.5-kilogram dumbbells; one yoga mat
interval_guidance: low-impact intervals 1x per week after clearance
interval_examples: 6×30s hard / 90s easy on bike or incline walk, or 20s hard/40s easy bodyweight/dumbbell circuit
tempo_guidance: BPM ranges by session type (Warm-up 100–120, Strength 80–110, Steady-state cardio 110–130, Low-impact intervals 140–160, Cool-down 60–90)

playlist_picks_by_session_type: Warm-up: mellow electronic / mid-tempo pop 100–120 BPM; Strength: groove-based rock / R&B 80–110 BPM; Steady cardio: upbeat pop / house 110–130 BPM; Low-impact intervals: high-energy electronic or modern pop 140–160 BPM; Cool-down: ambient / acoustic 60–90 BPM

safety_notes: Avoid very high-tempo music that tempts you into jump/run given knee restriction; prioritize controlled breathing and form; if matching tempo causes breath-holding or form issues, slow the tempo; use tempo as cue, not rule
off_day_walks: brisk walks 20–45 minutes, 2–6 times per week, RPE 4–6 (talk test), optional HR 50–70% of max
apps: Gymboss Interval Timer, FitNotes, Strong, Seconds
open: Primary goal(s) (strength, fat loss, general fitness, mobility, etc.)
Is your knee currently painful/swollen or limited in range?
Has your surgeon cleared you for strength/weightbearing exercises (squats, lunges, step-ups)?
Do you want three different workouts each week or the same workout repeated 3×?
Breathing guidance during strength work to avoid lightheadedness
